limitations: Stage 2 cross-platform consistency was not executed.
Local runtime test execution was attempted but stopped at dependency precondition resolution.
The v1.1.2 score is based on source, documentation, CI definitions, and C1-C4 LOCAL_ANALYSIS evidence.

rationale: The repository produces viral consensus and variant artifacts from sequencing reads.
No autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, or direct patient-management claim was observed in the locally reviewed files.
The codebase is present and substantive, so the CA-DIRECT plus zero-code T0 hard floor is not applicable.

score.stage_2_policy: N/A is not scored as zero; fixed v1.1.2 redistribution applies.
score.use_scope: Research reference and supervised non-clinical technical review only; no autonomous clinical decision support.

stage_1_evidence.finding: README describes a viral nanopore sequencing pipeline generated from tiling amplicon schemes.
README lists read filtering, primer trimming, amplicon normalisation, variant calling, and consensus building.
No explicit not-for-diagnostic-use or not-for-clinical-use boundary was observed in the README surface reviewed.

T1_CI_CD.evidence: .github/workflows/unittests.yml runs environment setup, package install, model download, unit tests, pipeline test runner, and minion validator.

T2_domain_tests.evidence: docs/tests.md documents full viral pipeline testing and slow SARS-CoV-2 variant/consensus validation against truth sets.

T3_changelog_release_hygiene.evidence: Repository contains CHANGELOG and release-oriented Docker build/push workflow.

T4_governance_maturity.evidence: No bounded governance overlay artifact was observed in the local review.

B1_data_provenance_controls.evidence: artic/utils.py includes manifest download and MD5 hash verification for scheme/reference artifacts; dependency lock/reproducibility remains incomplete.

B2_bias_limitations.evidence: No explicit bias, cohort representativeness, or biological limitation policy was observed.

B3_coi_funding.evidence: No COI/funding declaration was observed in the locally reviewed files.

evidence: .github/workflows/docker-build-push.yml:45-46 reference QUAY_USERNAME and QUAY_PASSWORD via GitHub secrets
Most other listed tools are unpinned.
artic/deprecated/filter_clusters/tagfastas.py:65 formats lab/sample/location/date metadata into FASTA record IDs.
artic/vcf_filter.py:42-49 fails closed when AF is missing.
artic/vcf_filter.py:63-71 swallows missing sample-level DP and can still return True if other filters pass.

runtime_evidence_boundary.interpretation: This is not completed runtime verification. It records the boundary of the local audit environment while preserving the v1.1.2 source/document/CI/C1-C4 evidence basis.
runtime_evidence_boundary.future_lane: Dependency-provisioned runtime replay should attach command, environment, exit-code, and output-digest evidence as a separate audit lane.

notable_positive_evidence: README claims align with actual package metadata and command-line entry points in pyproject.toml.
CI includes unit tests, pipeline test runner, and validator path.
docs/tests.md documents domain-specific variant and consensus validation against truth sets.
docs/quick-start.md warns that the pipeline requires tiling amplicon reads and is not meaningful for metagenomic or whole-genome shotgun data.
artic/utils.py verifies downloaded primer/reference artifacts against manifest hashes.
notable_risks: No explicit clinical-use or diagnostic-use boundary was observed in the README.
Dependencies are not fully pinned, limiting reproducibility of local scientific runs.
Deprecated code contains patient-adjacent metadata literals.
Sample-level missing DP in VCF filtering is not treated as a fail-closed condition.
